source: San Diego County Assessor, Prop 13 (California Constitution Article XIIIA)

baseRate.description: Proposition 13 base rate — 1% of assessed value
baseRate.notes: Assessed value is set at the purchase price and can only increase by a maximum of 2% per year under Prop 13. Reassessment to current market value occurs upon sale or new construction.

voterApprovedBonds.description: Additional taxes from voter-approved general obligation bonds for schools, infrastructure, and community facilities
voterApprovedBonds.notes: Varies by tax rate area (TRA). School bonds are the most common. These appear as separate line items on the tax bill.

directAssessments.description: Fixed-dollar assessments for services like flood control, vector control, library, fire, lighting, and landscaping districts
directAssessments.notes: These are flat fees, not percentages. They vary by location and the districts your property falls within.

melloRoos.description: Community Facilities District (CFD) taxes — most common in newer developments and master-planned communities
melloRoos.notes: Mello-Roos taxes fund infrastructure (roads, schools, parks, utilities) in new developments. They are a fixed annual amount, not based on assessed value, and typically last 25-40 years. Common in areas like Otay Ranch, Pacific Highlands Ranch, 4S Ranch, and San Marcos developments. Can add $2,000-$5,000+/year to your tax bill.

effectiveRates.description: Total effective property tax rate including base rate, bonds, and assessments (excluding Mello-Roos)

byAreaType.label: Older Established Neighborhood
byAreaType.notes: Minimal voter-approved bond overlay. No Mello-Roos. These areas were built out before CFDs became common.

byAreaType.label: Suburban / Mid-Age Development
byAreaType.notes: Moderate bond overlays. Some areas may have older Mello-Roos districts nearing expiration.

byAreaType.label: New Master-Planned Development
byAreaType.notes: Higher bond overlays plus significant Mello-Roos (CFD) taxes. The effective rate shown includes an estimated Mello-Roos equivalent. Actual Mello-Roos amounts are fixed dollars, not percentages, but are shown here as an equivalent rate for comparison.

calculationNotes: To estimate annual property taxes: (Purchase Price x Effective Rate) + Mello-Roos (if applicable) + Direct Assessments. Example: $700,000 home in Mira Mesa = $700,000 x 1.25% = $8,750/year. A $700,000 home in Otay Ranch = $700,000 x 1.10% + $3,500 Mello-Roos = $11,200/year.
